caption: Tenet turnaround under a new outside-hire CEO: ~$450M enterprise cost-out and roughly tripled adjusted EPS 2017-2019 ahead of the Conifer review.
Conifer 2018 auction: teasers to 74 parties, 9 bids, 3 finalists; buyers guided to normalized 2018 EBITDA of $308M, offers judged inadequate.
Planned Conifer spin (RemainCo 65 hospitals + 500 outpatient vs SpinCo Conifer, ~$25B net patient revenue managed) - a value-unlock later abandoned then reversed.
Tenet scale snapshot: ~$18.5B 2019 revenue, 65 hospitals, 570+ care facilities, ~50 USPI system partners, 113K staff, ~$25B Conifer-managed revenue.
Old three-segment view: 4Q19 Conifer segment adjusted-EBITDA margin 28.3% - standalone Conifer economics now folded into Hospital Other revenues.
SurgCenter Development deal intent: mid-8x EBITDA-NCI multiple (~5x effective), $40-50M synergies, positioning USPI as national musculoskeletal leader.
source_deck: 2020-12-10 · Expanding Ambulatory Surgery Portfolio (SurgCenter Development acquisition)

caption: USPI platform economics: 10,000+ physicians, 4,000+ physician partners, 50+ health-system partners, ~6% same-store growth, ~40% margin - the JV-partner scale metric.
source_deck: 2020-12-10 · Expanding Ambulatory Surgery Portfolio (SurgCenter Development acquisition)

caption: USPI leading ASC platform: ~310 centers and 24 surgical hospitals across 33 states before the 45-center SCD addition.
source_deck: 2020-12-10 · Expanding Ambulatory Surgery Portfolio (SurgCenter Development acquisition)

caption: SCD acquisition returns framing: ~6.9x EBITDA-NCI multiple by year 3, ~10% ROIC by year 3, ~28% EPS accretion by year 1.
source_deck: 2020-12-10 · Expanding Ambulatory Surgery Portfolio (SurgCenter Development acquisition)

caption: The strategic pivot in one chart: Ambulatory rose from ~4% of Tenet adjusted EBITDA (2014) to ~42% projected (2021E).
source_deck: 2020-12-10 · Expanding Ambulatory Surgery Portfolio (SurgCenter Development acquisition)

caption: USPI growth engine: 4-year case CAGR Spine 23.7% / Ortho 11.2%, total joints +116% YoY, robotics-assisted cases +49%, 45 new service lines in 1H21.
Turnaround scorecard 2017-2023E: adjusted-EBITDA margin 12.8% to 16.7%, leverage 5.9x to 4.14x, and five hospital markets (~$2.5B revenue, ~4% margin) divested.
FY2023 EBITDA bridge itemizing government-funding sensitivity: -$215M of reductions (Medicare add-on, 340B, FMAP, sequestration, DSH) plus a $100M cyber reversal.
USPI 2022 case mix: Musculoskeletal 36%, Gastrointestinal 34%, Ophthalmology 11% - the baseline before GI overtook MSK.
USPI roll-up returns: facilities 267 to 469 since 2017 at an 8.0x initial / sub-5x effective acquisition multiple and sub-2x de novo multiple.
Capital-deployment framework: ~$250M/yr ASC M&A and de novo baseline, hospital technology/robotics, deleveraging, and share repurchase.
Debt maturity profile at 6/30/2023 across first-lien, second-lien and unsecured notes - no significant maturities until 2026.
The 2025 breakout quarter: 19% consolidated adjusted-EBITDA growth, 21.3% margin, 74% adjusted-EPS growth, and a $395M full-year outlook raise.
FY2026 EBITDA bridge: 2025 Hospital normalized down $148M of one-off supplemental Medicaid, less $250M ACA premium-tax-credit expiration, plus $427M growth/efficiencies.
Conifer-CommonSpirit transaction mechanics: ~$1.65B contract-termination revenue (excluded from adjusted EBITDA), $540M to redeem CHI's stake, ~$100M lower NCI, ~$500M tax.
USPI 2019-2025 track record: net revenue $2,158M to $5,172M (15.7% CAGR), adjusted EBITDA $895M to $2,026M (14.6% CAGR), ~40% margins.
USPI organic-growth consistency: 6.2% same-facility system-wide revenue CAGR 2015-2025, with the -5.7% 2020 pandemic trough and +14.5% rebound.
USPI 2025 case mix: Gastrointestinal now largest at 42%, Musculoskeletal 30%, Ophthalmology 11% - GI overtook MSK versus 2022.
USPI roll-up returns updated: 267 to 559 facilities since 2017 at 8-10x initial / 5-7x effective acquisition multiples (realized over ~3 years).
FY2025 capital allocation: 29 ASCs acquired + 6 de novos ($339M M&A), $1.39B buyback (8.8M shares), and a refinancing pushing maturities to late 2027.
Deleveraging 2017-2025 on both bases: net leverage 5.86x to 2.25x on EBITDA and 6.95x to 2.85x on EBITDA-less-NCI (the ~0.6x gap is the USPI minority claim).
Q1 2026 highlights: ~$2.6B of value attributed to the Conifer-CommonSpirit transaction, SW&B 40.5% of revenue, Medicaid directed-payment revenue $22M below prior year.
